demo.title: harness-collaboration.sh
demo.code:
# This skill activates automatically when a Harness-delegated thread begins with:
#
#   HARNESS COLLABORATION PROTOCOL v1
#   External session: <id>
#   Limits hash: <hash>
#
# The agent then verifies the brief with its own research, reports through
# authenticated callbacks, and proposes before any side effect:

curl -X POST "$CALLBACK_URL" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CALLBACK_TOKEN" \
  -H "content-type: application/json" \
  -d '{"type": "progress", "eventId": "prog-1", "payload": {"message": "Verified the brief: liquidity checks out on Base"}}'

# Side effects execute only on a one-use Harness authorization turn
# bound to the exact proposal — never on conversational approval.

setup: For the agent behind a Harness-provisioned Bankr wallet — no manual setup; Harness delegates objectives to it directly
Activates when a prompt begins with the `HARNESS COLLABORATION PROTOCOL v1` header; the live prompt is always authoritative
Requires `curl` for authenticated callback delivery (progress, question, proposal, artifact, action_result, completed, failed)
Every side effect needs a proposal callback first, then a one-use authorization turn from Harness — caps are enforced server-side
Managing the wallet from a user's main Bankr account is the separate `harness` skill; this is the execution side
